member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.IDefaultApiSync.AnalyzeRecipe(spoonacular.Model.AnalyzeRecipeRequest,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Analyze Recipe
member.remarks: This endpoint allows you to send raw recipe information, such as title, servings, and ingredients, to then see what we compute (badges, diets, nutrition, and more). This is useful if you have your own recipe data and want to enrich it with our semantic analysis.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: Example request body.
The input language, either \"en\" or \"de\". (optional)
Whether nutrition data should be added to correctly parsed ingredients. (optional)
Whether taste data should be added to correctly parsed ingredients. (optional)
Index associated with the operation.

member.summary: Compute Ingredient Amount

member.param: The id of the ingredient you want the amount for.
The target nutrient. See a list of supported nutrients.
The target number of the given nutrient.
The target unit. (optional)

member.summary: Ingredient Search
member.remarks: Search for simple whole foods (e.g. fruits, vegetables, nuts, grains, meat, fish, dairy etc.).
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The (natural language) search query.
Whether to add children of found foods. (optional)
The minimum percentage of protein the food must have (between 0 and 100). (optional)
The maximum percentage of protein the food can have (between 0 and 100). (optional)
The minimum percentage of fat the food must have (between 0 and 100). (optional)
The maximum percentage of fat the food can have (between 0 and 100). (optional)
The minimum percentage of carbs the food must have (between 0 and 100). (optional)
The maximum percentage of carbs the food can have (between 0 and 100). (optional)
Whether to return more meta information about the ingredients. (optional)

A comma-separated list of intolerances. All recipes returned must not contain ingredients that are not suitable for people with the intolerances entered. See a full list of supported intolerances. (optional)
The strategy to sort recipes by. See a full list of supported sorting options. (optional)
The direction in which to sort. Must be either 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending). (optional)
The number of results to skip (between 0 and 900). (optional)
The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10. (optional, default to 10)
The language of the input. Either 'en' or 'de'. (optional)
Index associated with the operation.

The number of results to skip (between 0 and 900). (optional)

The maximum amount of carbohydrates in grams the menu item can have. (optional)

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.IMiscApiSync.DetectFoodInText(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Detect Food in Text
member.remarks: Take any text and find all mentions of food contained within it. This task is also called Named Entity Recognition (NER). In this case, the entities are foods. Either dishes, such as pizza or cheeseburger, or ingredients, such as cucumber or almonds.

member.param: Index associated with the operation.

member.summary: Talk to Chatbot This endpoint can be used to have a conversation about food with the spoonacular chatbot. Use the \"Get Conversation Suggests\" endpoint to show your user what he or she can say.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The request / question / answer from the user to the chatbot.
An arbitrary globally unique id for your conversation. The conversation can contain states so you should pass your context id if you want the bot to be able to remember the conversation. (optional)
Index associated with the operation.

member.param: The recipe's instructions.

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.IRecipesApiSync.ExtractRecipeFromWebsite(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Extract Recipe from Website
member.remarks: This endpoint lets you extract recipe data such as title, ingredients, and instructions from any properly formatted Website.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The URL of the recipe page.
If true, the extraction will be triggered whether we already know the recipe or not. Use this only if information is missing as this operation is slower. (optional)
If true, the recipe will be analyzed and classified resolving in more data such as cuisines, dish types, and more. (optional)

Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings. (optional, default to false)
Whether taste data should be added to correctly parsed ingredients. (optional, default to false)
Index associated with the operation.

member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call

Normalize to the strongest taste. (optional)
Red, green, blue values for the chart color. (optional)

member.summary: Search Recipes
member.remarks: Search through hundreds of thousands of recipes using advanced filtering and ranking. NOTE: This method combines searching by query, by ingredients, and by nutrients into one endpoint.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The (natural language) search query.
The cuisine(s) of the recipes. One or more, comma separated (will be interpreted as 'OR'). See a full list of supported cuisines. (optional)
The cuisine(s) the recipes must not match. One or more, comma separated (will be interpreted as 'AND'). See a full list of supported cuisines. (optional)
The diet for which the recipes must be suitable. See a full list of supported diets. (optional)

A comma-separated list of intolerances. All recipes returned must not contain ingredients that are not suitable for people with the intolerances entered. See a full list of supported intolerances. (optional)
The equipment required. Multiple values will be interpreted as 'or'. For example, value could be \"blender, frying pan, bowl\". (optional)
A comma-separated list of ingredients that should/must be used in the recipes. (optional)
A comma-separated list of ingredients or ingredient types that the recipes must not contain. (optional)
The type of recipe. See a full list of supported meal types. (optional)
Whether the recipes must have instructions. (optional)
Add information about the ingredients and whether they are used or missing in relation to the query. (optional)
If set to true, you get more information about the recipes returned. (optional)
If set to true, you get nutritional information about each recipes returned. (optional)
The username of the recipe author. (optional)
The tags (can be diets, meal types, cuisines, or intolerances) that the recipe must have. (optional)
The id of the recipe box to which the search should be limited to. (optional)
Enter text that must be found in the title of the recipes. (optional)
The maximum time in minutes it should take to prepare and cook the recipe. (optional)
The minimum amount of servings the recipe is for. (optional)
The maximum amount of servings the recipe is for. (optional)
Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc. (optional, default to false)
The strategy to sort recipes by. See a full list of supported sorting options. (optional)
The direction in which to sort. Must be either 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending). (optional)
The minimum amount of carbohydrates in grams the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of carbohydrates in grams the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of protein in grams the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of protein in grams the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of calories the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of calories the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of fat in grams the recipe must have. (optional)

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.IRecipesApiSync.SearchRecipesByIngredients(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Search Recipes by Ingredients
member.remarks: Ever wondered what recipes you can cook with the ingredients you have in your fridge or pantry? This endpoint lets you find recipes that either maximize the usage of ingredients you have at hand (pre shopping) or minimize the ingredients that you don't currently have (post shopping).
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: A comma-separated list of ingredients that the recipes should contain.
The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10. (optional, default to 10)
Whether to maximize used ingredients (1) or minimize missing ingredients (2) first. (optional)
Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc. (optional, default to false)
Index associated with the operation.

member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call

If true, the extraction will be triggered whether we already know the recipe or not. Use this only if information is missing as this operation is slower. (optional)
If true, the recipe will be analyzed and classified resolving in more data such as cuisines, dish types, and more. (optional)

Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings. (optional, default to false)
Whether taste data should be added to correctly parsed ingredients. (optional, default to false)

Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.summary: Search Recipes
member.remarks: Search through hundreds of thousands of recipes using advanced filtering and ranking. NOTE: This method combines searching by query, by ingredients, and by nutrients into one endpoint.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The (natural language) search query.
The cuisine(s) of the recipes. One or more, comma separated (will be interpreted as 'OR'). See a full list of supported cuisines. (optional)
The cuisine(s) the recipes must not match. One or more, comma separated (will be interpreted as 'AND'). See a full list of supported cuisines. (optional)
The diet for which the recipes must be suitable. See a full list of supported diets. (optional)

A comma-separated list of intolerances. All recipes returned must not contain ingredients that are not suitable for people with the intolerances entered. See a full list of supported intolerances. (optional)
The equipment required. Multiple values will be interpreted as 'or'. For example, value could be \"blender, frying pan, bowl\". (optional)
A comma-separated list of ingredients that should/must be used in the recipes. (optional)
A comma-separated list of ingredients or ingredient types that the recipes must not contain. (optional)
The type of recipe. See a full list of supported meal types. (optional)
Whether the recipes must have instructions. (optional)
Add information about the ingredients and whether they are used or missing in relation to the query. (optional)
If set to true, you get more information about the recipes returned. (optional)
If set to true, you get nutritional information about each recipes returned. (optional)
The username of the recipe author. (optional)
The tags (can be diets, meal types, cuisines, or intolerances) that the recipe must have. (optional)
The id of the recipe box to which the search should be limited to. (optional)
Enter text that must be found in the title of the recipes. (optional)
The maximum time in minutes it should take to prepare and cook the recipe. (optional)
The minimum amount of servings the recipe is for. (optional)
The maximum amount of servings the recipe is for. (optional)
Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc. (optional, default to false)
The strategy to sort recipes by. See a full list of supported sorting options. (optional)
The direction in which to sort. Must be either 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending). (optional)
The minimum amount of carbohydrates in grams the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of carbohydrates in grams the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of protein in grams the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of protein in grams the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of calories the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of calories the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of fat in grams the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of fat in grams the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of alcohol in grams the recipe must have. (optional)
The maximum amount of alcohol in grams the recipe can have. (optional)
The minimum amount of caffeine in milligrams the recipe must have. (optional)

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.IRecipesApiAsync.SearchRecipesByIngredientsAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Search Recipes by Ingredients
member.remarks: Ever wondered what recipes you can cook with the ingredients you have in your fridge or pantry? This endpoint lets you find recipes that either maximize the usage of ingredients you have at hand (pre shopping) or minimize the ingredients that you don't currently have (post shopping).
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: A comma-separated list of ingredients that the recipes should contain.
The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10. (optional, default to 10)
Whether to maximize used ingredients (1) or minimize missing ingredients (2) first. (optional)
Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc. (optional, default to false)
Index associated with the operation.
Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

The author of the recipe. (optional)
The background color for the recipe card as a hex-string. (optional)
The font color for the recipe card as a hex-string. (optional)
The source of the recipe. (optional)

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.RecipesApi.ExtractRecipeFromWebsite(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Extract Recipe from Website This endpoint lets you extract recipe data such as title, ingredients, and instructions from any properly formatted Website.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The URL of the recipe page.
If true, the extraction will be triggered whether we already know the recipe or not. Use this only if information is missing as this operation is slower. (optional)
If true, the recipe will be analyzed and classified resolving in more data such as cuisines, dish types, and more. (optional)

Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings. (optional, default to false)
Whether taste data should be added to correctly parsed ingredients. (optional, default to false)
Index associated with the operation.

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.RecipesApi.SearchRecipesByIngredients(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Search Recipes by Ingredients Ever wondered what recipes you can cook with the ingredients you have in your fridge or pantry? This endpoint lets you find recipes that either maximize the usage of ingredients you have at hand (pre shopping) or minimize the ingredients that you don't currently have (post shopping).
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: A comma-separated list of ingredients that the recipes should contain.

member.name: M:spoonacular.Api.RecipesApi.SearchRecipesByIngredientsWithHttpInfo(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Search Recipes by Ingredients Ever wondered what recipes you can cook with the ingredients you have in your fridge or pantry? This endpoint lets you find recipes that either maximize the usage of ingredients you have at hand (pre shopping) or minimize the ingredients that you don't currently have (post shopping).
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: A comma-separated list of ingredients that the recipes should contain.
The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10. (optional, default to 10)
Whether to maximize used ingredients (1) or minimize missing ingredients (2) first. (optional)
Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc. (optional, default to false)
Index associated with the operation.

member.name: P:spoonacular.Client.Configuration.ApiKeyPrefix
member.summary:
Gets or sets the prefix (e.g. Token) of the API key based on the authentication name.
Whatever you set here will be prepended to the value defined in AddApiKey.
An example invocation here might be:
ApiKeyPrefix["Authorization"] = "Bearer";
… where ApiKey["Authorization"] would then be used to set the value of your bearer token.
OAuth2 workflows should set tokens via AccessToken.
member.value: The prefix of the API key.
